success_surface: Cell-level formula application with cross-platform compatibility
effective_behaviors: Verified file integrity and created backup before any modifications
Used openpyxl for direct formula writing instead of pandas dataframes
Wrote formulas as raw strings with dynamic cell references that adjust per row during iteration
Targeted exact cell range specified in answer_position without extending beyond bounds
Selected formula based on target platform compatibility (Google Sheets constraints)
Provided clear execution feedback including formula used and affected cells
evidence: Created backup copy using shutil.copy before loading workbook
Applied formula as string: f'=ROUNDUP(E{row}/1000, 0)' allowing E column reference to change per row
Loop ran exactly for rows 2-6 matching answer_position F2:F6 specification
Chose ROUNDUP over XLOOKUP due to Google Sheets requirement stated in instruction
Printed success confirmation with formula documentation

memory_items.title: Formula Platform Compatibility Check
memory_items.lesson: When creating formulas, verify they are compatible with the target spreadsheet platform (Excel, Google Sheets, etc.) before implementation.
memory_items.generalization: Always consider platform-specific function restrictions when selecting formulas; choose universally supported functions when cross-platform compatibility is required.

memory_items.title: Dynamic Cell Reference Construction
memory_items.lesson: When applying formulas across multiple rows, use variable cell references that adjust per iteration rather than hardcoding absolute references.
memory_items.generalization: In loop-based formula application, construct formula strings with row/column variables so each cell receives its appropriate relative reference.

memory_items.title: Backup Verification Before Modification
memory_items.lesson: Create a file copy before any spreadsheet operations to preserve original data state.
memory_items.generalization: Always implement backup creation as first step before write operations on spreadsheet files to enable rollback if needed.

memory_items.title: Exact Range Adherence
memory_items.lesson: Modify only the cell range specified in the task requirements without extending beyond.
memory_items.generalization: Respect the defined target scope precisely; avoid unnecessary operations on cells outside the specified range.

edits.content:
## 6. Formula-Specific Guidelines
- **Platform Compatibility**: Choose formulas compatible with the target platform (Excel, Google Sheets, etc.). Avoid platform-specific functions when cross-platform support is needed.
- **Dynamic References in Loops**: When applying formulas across multiple cells via iteration, construct formula strings with variable row/column references (e.g., `f'={func}(A{row})'`) so each cell gets appropriate relative reference.
- **String-Only Formula Storage**: Always store formulas as raw strings with '=' prefix. Never pre-evaluate formulas before writing them.
- **Range Precision**: Apply formulas only to the specified target range. Do not extend beyond answer_position boundaries unless explicitly required.
